title: C# Basic — Paper 5
description: C# concepts: operators, conditionals, switch expressions, loops, loop control, classes, constructors, access modifiers, properties, and static members.

questions.text: Which operator checks both the reference AND value equality in C# for most primitive types?

options.text: Equals()
ReferenceEquals()

questions.text: What is the result of `~5` (bitwise NOT) in C# for a 32-bit integer?
What is the associativity of the assignment operators (`=`, `+=`, `-=`) in C#?

options.text: Left-to-right
Non-associative
Right-to-left
They have no associativity
big
small
medium
No output

questions.text: Which C# statement is a valid `switch` with a `string` variable?

options.text: switch (color) { case "red": break; case "blue": break; }
switch (color) { case color == "red": break; }
switch on color { "red" -> break; }
C# does not support string in switch

questions.text: What is the purpose of the `goto` statement in C#?

options.text: It is not supported in C#
It transfers control to a labeled statement elsewhere in the code
It exits a loop immediately
It calls a method by name

questions.text: What is a relational pattern in C# switch expressions?

options.text: A pattern that matches a range using operators like `< 0` or `>= 100`
A pattern that tests if a type has a certain property
A pattern that checks if an object is null
A pattern that uses regex to match strings

questions.text: What does `obj is not null` check in C# pattern matching?

options.text: It is invalid syntax
It checks that obj is of a specific type
It checks that obj is not null (null negation pattern)
It converts obj to a non-nullable type

questions.text: What does a `switch` expression do when no arm matches and there is no `_` discard arm?

options.text: Returns the default value of the result type
Throws a SwitchExpressionException at runtime
Falls through to the end without returning a value
Causes a compile-time error

questions.text: How many times does the body of `for (int i = 5; i > 0; i--)` execute?
What happens if you modify the collection variable inside a `foreach` loop in C#?

options.text: The loop sees the modified values immediately
Nothing — the iteration variable is read-only inside the loop body
A runtime InvalidOperationException is thrown
The loop variable changes but the collection is unchanged

questions.text: What is the minimum number of times a `do-while` body executes?

options.text: Depends on the condition

questions.text: What does `continue` do inside a loop in C#?

options.text: Exits the loop immediately
Skips the rest of the current iteration and moves to the next one
Restarts the loop from the beginning
Pauses execution until the condition changes

questions.text: What is the behavior of `return` inside a loop in C#?

options.text: It exits only the loop, not the method
It exits the method entirely, returning a value if specified
It continues with the next iteration
It throws an exception

questions.text: How does `break` in a `switch` statement differ from `break` in a loop in C#?

options.text: They are identical in behavior
In a switch, `break` exits the case; in a loop, `break` exits the loop. Both exit their enclosing construct
`break` in a switch throws an exception; in a loop it just exits
`break` in a loop exits all loops; in a switch it exits only the switch

questions.text: What is the difference between a class field and a class property in C#?

options.text: Fields have accessors; properties are plain storage
Properties expose controlled access via get/set; fields are direct variable storage
Properties are always faster than fields
Fields are always public; properties are always private

questions.text: Can an object initializer call a specific constructor with arguments in C#?

options.text: No, object initializers always use the default constructor
Yes, you can use `new MyClass(arg1) { Prop = val }` to call a constructor with args and then set properties
No, constructors and object initializers are mutually exclusive
Yes, but only if the class is sealed

questions.text: What does `:this()` constructor chaining allow in C#?

options.text: One constructor to call the base class constructor
One constructor to call another constructor in the same class to avoid code duplication
A constructor to create a copy of itself
A derived class to call the original class constructor

questions.text: Can a class have multiple static constructors in C#?

options.text: Yes, up to two
Yes, unlimited
No, only one static constructor is allowed per class
Yes, but only if they have different parameter lists

questions.text: What does the `public` access modifier mean in C#?

options.text: Accessible only within the declaring class
Accessible only within the same assembly
Accessible from any code in any assembly
Accessible only to derived classes

questions.text: Which access modifier combination in C# allows access within the same assembly AND by derived classes in any assembly?

options.text: private protected
protected internal
public protected
internal protected

questions.text: What is the default access modifier for a class member in C# if none is specified?

options.text: public
internal
private
protected

questions.text: What does a property with only a `get` accessor (no `set`) represent in C#?

options.text: A write-only property
A read-only property that cannot be set after construction
A property with a default value
An auto-implemented property

questions.text: What is the difference between `{ get; set; }` and `{ get; init; }` in C# 9+?

options.text: `{ get; set; }` allows setting any time; `{ get; init; }` only allows setting during construction
`{ get; init; }` allows setting any time; `{ get; set; }` only allows setting once
They are functionally identical
`init` makes the property static

questions.text: What can a property's `set` accessor do that a field assignment cannot easily do?

options.text: Store a value in memory
Execute validation logic or raise change notifications before storing the value
Allow the value to change
Prevent assignment of null

questions.text: What is a static field in C#?

options.text: A field declared inside a method
A field shared by all instances of a class, belonging to the class itself rather than any object
A read-only field initialized at declaration
A field that cannot be modified once set

questions.text: What is a static method in C#?

options.text: A method that can only be called once
A method belonging to the class rather than an instance; called via the class name
A method that does not return a value
A method that cannot have parameters

questions.text: Can a static method access instance (non-static) fields of the class directly in C#?

options.text: Yes, static methods have full access to all members
No, static methods can only access static members unless given an object instance
Yes, but only if the field is public
Yes, but only through the `base` keyword

questions.text: What is the purpose of a static class in C#?

options.text: To allow only one instance to be created (singleton)
To prevent the class from being inherited
To group utility methods and constants that don't require instance state; cannot be instantiated
To mark all members as thread-safe

questions.text: How do you call a static method `GetMax` from a static class `MathHelper` in C#?

options.text: new MathHelper().GetMax(a, b);
MathHelper helper = new MathHelper(); helper.GetMax(a, b);
MathHelper.GetMax(a, b);
static MathHelper.GetMax(a, b);
